summary: High‑energy Mardi Gras set with killer Hand Jive and tight jams
blurb: The show bursts with a party atmosphere, highlighted by the first‑ever Neville Brothers Hand Jive and fiery renditions of Cassidy and Johnny B. Goode. Jerry’s crisp lead work and Brent’s driving keyboards power the second‑set drums battle, while the encore U.S. Blues caps a tight performance.
review: On February 12, 1986 at the Henry J. Kaiser Convention Center the Dead turned the night into a Mardi Gras party. From the opening “Hell In A Bucket” through a bright “Sugaree,” they built momentum that exploded in the second set. The highlight was the historic collaboration with the Neville Brothers on “Willie And The Hand Jive**,” where Mickey and Bill’s drums roared louder than ever, propelled by Brent’s punchy keys. Jerry Garcia cut a crystal‑clear tone, leading the “Hand Jive” and a scorching “Johnny B. Goode*,” while Phil Lesh laid down a solid foundation on “Cassidy.” Bob Weir’s vocal fire kept the crowd engaged, and despite a brief wobble in the “Estimated Prophet,” the band stayed locked in. The night closed with an upbeat “U.S. Blues” that left the audience buzzing long after the curfew‑cut set ended.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the energetic second set, the Hand Jive with Neville Brothers, and standout jams like Cassidy and Johnny B. Goode. No negative comments about performance quality appear; only minor notes of early drum off‑kilter moments and a slip in Estimated Prophet, which are outweighed by the overall enthusiasm. This consistent high praise warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, flawless audio that showcases the energetic performance and especially the standout drum battle and jam sections.

key_highlights: Neville Brothers Hand Jive (Willie And The Hand Jive**) electrifies the second set.
Brent's keyboards drive the extended Drums Part II, adding extra propulsion.
Tight jams on Cassidy and Johnny B. Goode with Jerry’s soaring lead work.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers crystal‑clear tone and leads the high‑energy jams, especially on Hand Jive and Johnny B. Goode; a minor stumble in Estimated Prophet but otherwise on point.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation throughout, stepping up notably during Cassidy.
band_performance.Bob: Adds energetic vocals and stage presence, spitting to the crowd and contributing strong rhythm work during the jams.
band_performance.Brent: Shines on keyboards, particularly during Drums Part II with the Neville Brothers, adding drive and color to the rhythm section.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are a highlight; despite an early off‑kilter feel, the extended drum segment with the Nevilles is louder, faster, and a major high point of the show.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey to create a powerful percussion wall; his work on the Hand Jive and second‑set drums battle is especially praised.
